California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1989

Michele C. Kibbe-Chairs
General Counsel
Oakland Unified School District
1025 Second Avenue, Room 406
Oakland, CA 94606

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-89-251

Dear Ms. Kibbe-Chairs:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Oakland Unified School District concerning the application of the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act") to the mailings produced and distributed by your
school district.1/ Since your request does not refer to a
specific governmental decision and is of a general nature, we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.2/

In your letter you requested all the "compliance guidelines,
regulations and interpretations developed regarding Government
Code Section 89001" in relation to your question concerning the
Oakland Unified School District's mail procedures. The Commission
has adopted Regulation 18901 to implement and interpret the mass
mailing restrictions of Section 89001. A copy is enclosed for
your reference.

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-89-251
Page 2

QUESTION

May the Oakland Unified School District produce and
distribute at public expense informational materials which include
the names, pictures, signatures of or references to elected school
board members?

CONCLUSION

Any use of a school board member's name, photograph or any
reference to a member is prohibited if the mailing is initiated,
directed or controlled by the member, or is signed by or credited
to the member. In addition, any use of a member's name,
photograph, office or any reference to the board member is
prohibited if the member is involved in the production or
distribution of the mailing. If the school board members do not
participate in the preparation or distribution of the mailing,
then use of the board members' names is permitted provided they
are not featured in the mailing.

DISCUSSION

Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mail-
ing shall be sent at public expense. Section 82041.5 defines a
"mass mailing" as more than 200 substantially similar pieces of
mail sent in a calendar month, but excludes mailings sent in
response to a specific, unsolicited request. Regulation 18901(c)
provides that a newsletter or other mass mailing may not be sent
within the meaning of Section 89001 if:

(1) The name of the elected officer or
his or her photograph appears on the document;
and

(A) The elected officer exercises
direction or control over the content,
production, or distribution of the
document, or

(B) The document is sent at the
request or suggestion of the elected of-
ficer or his or her agent; or

(C) The document is signed by, or
is designated as being from, the elected
officer or his or her office; or

(2) (A) The elected officer is af-
filiated with the agency which produces or
distributes the document; and

(i) The elected officer is
featured in the document; or

File No. I-89-251
Page 3

(ii) The name, office or other
reference to the elected officer or
his or her photograph appears on the
document and the document is
prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or
concert with the elected officer.

Thus, where the elected officer initiates the mailing,
exercises direction or control over the content, production, or
distribution of the document, or signs or takes credit for the
document and the name of the elected officer or photograph appears
in the document, it is prohibited under Section 89001. Moreover,
where an elected official is affiliated with an agency that
produces or distributes the mass mailing, the restrictions of
Section 89001 may still apply in some circumstances.

An elected official is affiliated with an agency that
produces and distributes a mass mailing if the elected official is
a member, officer or employee of the agency or a subunit (such as
a committee) of that agency; has supervisory control over the
agency; or, appoints any of the members of the agency. (Riddle
Advice Letter, No. A-89-096, copy enclosed.) Clearly, members of
the governing board of the school district are "affiliated" with
the district, which produces or distributes the mass mailing.

However, affiliation alone does not make the use of an
elected official's name in a mass mailing prohibited. The second
question is whether the mailing is prepared or sent in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the
elected officer. Where the mailing has been prepared or sent in
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concerted with the
elected officer, any use of the elected officer's name, photograph
or office, or any reference to the officer is prohibited.
(Regulation 18901(b)(2)(A)(ii).)

If the mailing has not been prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer,
use of the elected officer's name is permitted provided the
official is not featured in the mailing. (Regulation
18901(b)(2)(A)(i).) An elected officer is "featured" in a mass
mailing if he or she is singled out for attention of the reader by
use of his or her signature, inclusion in any photograph, or the
manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of
the document such as by headlines, type size, or typeface.
(Regulation 18901(b)(2)(B).)

Therefore, if the mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the school board
members, any use of the members' names, photographs, office, or
any references to the officeholders are prohibited. If the school
board members did not participate in the preparation or
distribution of the mailing, then use of the members' names is
permitted provided the members are not featured in the mailing.

File No. I-89-251
Page 4

In addition, Regulation 18901(e) sets out a specific
exception to the mass mailing prohibition. Regulation 18901(e)
permits the use of an elected official's name in the standard
letterhead or logotype of the stationery, forms and envelopes of
an agency. As used in this subdivision, the term "letterhead or
logotype" includes a listing of agency or committee officials or
members, in which all who are listed appear in the same typeface
and type size and location in the layout of the newsletter or
other mass mailing. (Regulation 18901(e)(2).)

Consequently, over 200 copies of the district mailings may be
sent at public expense provided there are no names, photographs or
references to elected officials, with the exception of a listing
of agency members where all the names are set out in the same
typeface, type size and location in the layout of the mass
mailing.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter or a
specific mailing that you would like advice on, please feel free
to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Chairman Larson:

     Please provide this office with any and all Commission
compliance guidelines, regulations and interpretations developed
regarding Government Code Section 89001.  Specifically, we need
to know if the names, pictures or signatures or any reference to
incumbent elected governing board members of a school district
may appear in District paid for publications which provide
information about programs, performance of programs, policies
and procedures, and/or other periodicals for mass distribution
to the public?

     Your prompt response to this request will be appreciated.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Michele C. Kibbe-Chairs
                                        General Counsel

Dear Chairman Larson:

     Please provide this office with any and all Commission
compliance guidelines, regulations and interpretations developed
regarding Government Code Section 89001. Specifically, we need
to know if the names, pictures or signatures of any elected to
incumbent elected governing board members of a school district
may appear in District paid for publications which provide
information about programs, performance of programs, policies
and procedures, and/or other periodicals for mass distribution
to the public?

     Your prompt response to this request will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Michele C. Kibbe-Chairs
General Counsel

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 2, 1989


Michele C. Kibbe-Chairs
General Counsel
Oakland Unified School District
1025 Second Avenue, Room 406
Oakland, CA 94606

Re: Letter No. 89-251

Dear Ms. Kibbe-Chairs:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 28, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Scott Hallabrin an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

Re: Request for Advice
Your File No. A-88-141

Dear Ms. Spitz:

We need further clarification on your response to the above
request. Please refer to the attached copy of my April 6th
letter. I was and am now again requesting clarification as
to the filing of all candidates' statements. The Registrar
of Voters forwarded to us the candidate statements for all
elected offices (see attached list and memo).

As you know, the declarations of candidacy are filed with
the Registrar of Voters and the Form 721's as indicated in
my April 6th letter are filed with this office. But, we
desire verification as to whether the candidate statements,
Forms 721, (see attached Final Candidate List for 1988
filings) should be filed with us, the Registrar of Voters,
or some other department.

We are requesting a letter of interpretation as to the
above.

If you require further information, please contact me at
(714) 387-3970.

Sincerely,

Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

SUPERIOR COURT/COUNTY CLERK                                 COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO

351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor, San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210 • (714) 387-3921
Main District, 8303 Haven Avenue, Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91730 • (714) 945-4136
Desert District, 14435 Civic Drive, Victorville, CA 92392 • (619) 243-8870
North Desert District, 235 E. Mountain View Avenue, Barstow, CA 92311 • (619) 256-4816


                            April 6, 1988




Mr. Bruce Robeck
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Mr. Robeck:

     Pursuant to our telephone conversation of April 1, 1988,
we are requesting a Letter of Interpretation for guidelines
as to where Candidate Statements' Forms 721's are to be
filed in San Bernardino County.

     The Registrar receives all other campaign/election documents
but feels that the Forms 721's are to be filed with the
County Clerk's Office.  Enclosed is a copy of a transmittal
memo from the Registrar of Voters to the Technical
Assistance and Analysis Division on this subject.

     The County Clerk's Office receives Forms 721 for the
Superior Court Judges, Commissioners, CAO, County Counsel,
District Attorney, Board of Supervisors and Planning
Commissioners.  After a review of our records for the past 3
years, we found no record of Candidate Statements being
received or filed in our office.

     Therefore, a Letter of Interpretation is requested at this
time.

     If you require further information, please contact me at
(714) 387-3970.

Sincerely,


Linda D. Hamilton
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

cc:Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

REGISTRAR OF VOTERS                                                COUNTY OF SAN BERNARD

777 East Rialto Avenue • San Bernardino, CA 92415-0770 • (714) 387-8300

                    March 28, 1988




TO: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

FROM: REGISTRAR OF VOTERS, SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY
       Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

RE: Candidates' Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721)

Confirming my telephone call with your office on March 14, and your
reply that information would be forthcoming in about a week with your
Memo on the handling of Form 721s. Your memo was received on
March 21 and we are forwarding 34 copies of Candidates' Form 721.

Your information that documents be date stamped was received after
the fact; also, inasmuch as the County Clerk is the filing officer,
we are also sending a copy there to be filed.

Please call A. Butler, 714/387-2082, if you have any questions or
if we can be of further service.

351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor
San Bernardino, CA 92515-0210

Re: 88-251

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on July 1, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

sections.question:
May the Oakland Unified school District produce and 
distribute at public expense informational materials which include 
the names, pictures, signatures of or references to elected school 
board members?
sections.conclusion:
Any use of a school board member's name, photograph or any 
reference to a member is prohibited if the mailing is initiated, 
directed or controlled by the member, or is signed by or credited 
to the member. 
In addition, any use of a member's name, 
photograph, office, or any reference to the board member is 
prohibited if the member is involved in the production or 
distribution of the mailing. 
If the school board members do not 
participate in the preparation or distribution of the mailing, 
then use of the board members' names is permitted provided they 
are not featured in the mailing.
sections.analysis:
section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mail-
ing shall be sent at public expense. 
section 82041.5 defines a 
"mass mailing" as more than 200 substantially similar pieces of 
mail sent in a calendar month, but excludes mailings sent in 
response to a specific, unsolicited request. 
Regulation 18901(c) 
provides that a newsletter or other mass mailing may not be sent 
within the meaning of section 89001 if: 
(1) 
The name of the elected officer or 
his or her photograph appears on the document; 
and 
(A) 
The elected officer exercises 
direction or control over the content, 
production, or distribution of the 
document, or 
(B) 
The document is sent at the 
request or suggestion of the elected of-
ficer or his or her agent; or 
(C) 
The document is signed by, or 
is designated as being from, the elected 
officer or his or her office; or 
(2) 
(A) 
The elected officer is af-
filiated with the agency which produces or 
distributes the document; and 
(i) 
The elected officer is 
featured in the document; or  
(ii) 
The name, office or other 
reference to the elected officer or 
his or her photograph appears on the 
document and the document is 
prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination or 
concert with the elected officer. 
Thus, where the elected officer initiates the mailing, 
exercises direction or control over the content, production, or 
distribution of the document, or signs or takes credit for the 
document and the name of the elected officer or photograph appears 
in the document, it is prohibited under section 89001. 
Moreover, 
where an elected official is affiliated with an agency that 
produces or distributes the mass mailing, the restrictions of 
Section 89001 may still apply in some circumstances. 
An elected official is affiliated with an agency that 
produces and distributes a mass mailing if the elected official is 
a member, officer or employee of the agency or a subunit (such as 
a committee) of that agency; has supervisory control over the 
agency; or, appoints any of the members of the agency. 
(Riddle 
Advice Letter, No. A-89-096, copy enclosed.) 
Clearly, members of 
the governing board of the school district are "affiliated" with 
the district, which produces or distributes the mass mailing. 
However, affiliation alone does not make the use of an 
elected official's name in a mass mailing prohibited. 
The second 
question is whether the mailing is prepared or sent in 
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the 
elected officer. 
Where the mailing has been prepared or sent in 
cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with the 
elected officer, any use of the elected officer's name, photograph 
or office, or any reference to the officer is prohibited. 
(Regulation 18901(b) (2) (A) (ii).) 
If the mailing has not been prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer, 
use of the elected officer's name is permitted provided the 
official is not featured in the mailing. 
(Regulation 
18901(b) (2) (A) (i).) 
An elected officer is "featured" in a mass 
mailing if he or she is singled out for attention of the reader by 
use of his or her signature, inclusion in any photograph, or the 
manner of display of his or her name or office in the layout of 
the document such as by headlines, type size, or typeface. 
(Regulation 18901(b) (2) (8).) 
Therefore, if the mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, 
consultation, coordination or concert with the school board 
members, any use of the members' names, photographs, office, or 
any references to the officeholders are prohibited. 
If the school 
board members did not participate in the preparation or 
distribution of the mailing, then use of the members' names is 
permitted provided the members are not featured in the mailing.  
In addition, Regulation 18901(e) sets out a specific 
exception to the mass mailing prohibition. 
Regulation 18901(e) 
permits the use of an elected official's name in the standard 
letterhead or logotype of the stationery, forms and envelopes of 
an agency. 
As used in this subdivision, the term "letterhead or 
logotype" includes a listing of agency or committee officials or 
members, in which all who are listed appear in the same typeface 
and type size and location in the layout of the newsletter or 
other mass mailing. (Regulation 18901(e) (2).) 
Consequently, over 200 copies of the district mailings may be 
sent at public expense provided there are no names, photographs or 
references to elected officials, with the exception of a listing 
of agency members where all the names are set out in the same 
typeface, type size and location in the layout of the mass 
mailing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May the Oakland Unified school District produce and 
distribute at public expense informational materials which include 
the names, pictures, signatures of or references to elected school 
board members?

CONCLUSION: Any use of a school board member's name, photograph or any 
reference to a member is prohibited if the mailing is initiated, 
directed or controlled by the member, or is signed by or credited 
to the member. 
In addition, any use of a member's name, 
photograph, office, or any reference to the board member is 
prohibited if the member is involved in the production or 
distribution of the mailing. 
If the school board members do not 
participate in the preparation or distribution of the mailing, 
then use of the board members' names is permitted provided they 
are not featured in the mailing.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission July 18, 1989 Michele C. Kibbe-Chairs General Counsel Oakland Unified School District 1025 Second Avenue, Room 406 Oakland, CA 94606 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. I-89-251 Dear Ms. Kibbe-Chairs: This is in response to your letter requesting advice on behalf of the Oakland Unified School District concerning the application of the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") to the mailings produced and distributed by your school district.1/ Since your request does not refer to a specific governmental decision and is of a general nature, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2/ In your letter you requested all the "compliance guidelines, regulations and interpretations developed regarding Government Code Section 89001" in relation to your question concerning the Oakland Unified School District's mail procedures. The Commission has adopted Regulation 18901 to implement and interpret the mass mailing restrictions of Section 89001. A copy is enclosed for your reference. 1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 File No. I-89-251 Page 2 QUESTION May the Oakland Unified School District produce and distribute at public expense informational materials which include the names, pictures, signatures of or references to elected school board members? CONCLUSION Any use of a school board member's name, photograph or any reference to a member is prohibited if the mailing is initiated, directed or controlled by the member, or is signed by or credited to the member. In addition, any use of a member's name, photograph, office or any reference to the board member is prohibited if the member is involved in the production or distribution of the mailing. If the school board members do not participate in the preparation or distribution of the mailing, then use of the board members' names is permitted provided they are not featured in the mailing. DISCUSSION Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mail- ing shall be sent at public expense. Section 82041.5 defines a "mass mailing" as more than 200 substantially similar pieces of mail sent in a calendar month, but excludes mailings sent in response to a specific, unsolicited request. Regulation 18901(c) provides that a newsletter or other mass mailing may not be sent within the meaning of Section 89001 if: (1) The name of the elected officer or his or her photograph appears on the document; and (A) The elected officer exercises direction or control over the content, production, or distribution of the document, or (B) The document is sent at the request or suggestion
